methods.name: Peer-Median

methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
The Nuclear Company is a Series A frontier-tech venture in nuclear energy that has raised $50M+ to date. The broader nuclear energy sector includes 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, Kairos Power, X-Energy, and Oklo, though only a narrow subset operate at comparable stage and valuation tiers.

Peer-median valuation proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage datapoints within the nuclear energy peer set; a cross-sector median would introduce material distortion. Stage-progression analysis was therefore the primary triangulation method, applying a standard 2.0x Series A-to-next-stage step-up multiplier to the $50M total raised as a proxy base, yielding $100M implied value. This multiplier reflects typical venture capital progression mechanics in early-stage frontier tech.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $85M to $115M, with $100M as the median point. Key uncertainties include the applicability of standard step-up multiples to the nuclear energy sector specifically, where regulatory and commercialization timelines differ materially from software or biotech; the lack of narrow-peer comparable data; and the extent to which the founders' track record and proprietary technology justify variance from the stage-based baseline. A more robust conclusion would require either expansion of the same-stage nuclear peer set or sector-specific calibration data.
